codeReview.summary: ReviewerAgent was successfully enhanced with semantic code review capabilities. The implementation covers all expected behaviors: style analysis (trailing whitespace, long lines, TODOs), complexity analysis (cyclomatic complexity via nesting depth, long functions), convention checks (missing doc comments, debug printlns, unwrap usage), and LLM-based semantic review when configured. The agent properly extracts changed files from task plan, reads them via tool registry, analyzes them, and computes review scores and merge eligibility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' which does not exist in .factory/skills/. All workers in this mission have encountered this same issue (orch-state-machine, orch-execution-controller, orch-orchestrator-api, orch-task-graph, and this agents-reviewer-agent). Workers consistently proceed using 'Rust' skill or base development practices when the specified skill is unavailable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'The rust-worker skill does not exist. Let me check what skills are available' - same pattern observed in all other worker sessions in this mission.

summary: Feature implementation passes review. ReviewerAgent now performs actual semantic code review covering style, complexity, conventions, and LLM-based semantic analysis. The implementation matches the feature description and expected behaviors. Worker followed procedure (proceeding with Rust development when rust-worker skill wasn't found). No blocking issues identified.
